headlines.title: Legal advisers help migrants pose as gay to get asylum, undercover BBC investigation finds
headlines.description: The BBC exposes a shadow industry charging migrants thousands of pounds to help them cheat the asylum system.

headlines.title: Watch: Reporter told to pose as gay for asylum claim
headlines.description: A BBC reporter goes undercover and speaks to an adviser encouraging migrants to cheat the asylum system.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Iran war has trapped Labour in a vicious circle
headlines.description: A subdued economy makes trade-offs and choices over public spending more difficult, Chris Mason writes.

headlines.title: AA and BSM ordered to refund learner drivers for hidden fees
headlines.description: The owner of the driving schools has been fined for failing to disclose fees upfront online.

headlines.title: 'I'm not being listened to' - new health plan launched as women say they are still ignored
headlines.description: New plans to improve healthcare for women and girls have been set out, but will they change anything?

headlines.title: Australia's richest person must share part of her mining fortunes, court rules
headlines.description: The long-running legal dispute is over a lucrative iron ore project in the top west corner of Australia.

headlines.title: Butterfly numbers are dropping but here are five species you may see more of
headlines.description: A warming climate has helped some to flourish, researchers say, but the outlook is troubling.

headlines.title: Help to Buy mostly helped high earners, IFS says
headlines.description: People with lower incomes benefitted less from the house-buying scheme than those with high incomes, the influential think tank says.

headlines.title: Magyar meets Hungarian president as Trump says next PM 'a good man'
headlines.description: The man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, Péter Magyar, is calling for a speedy transfer of power.

headlines.title: Two more men charged with Primrose Hill murder
headlines.description: Finbar Sullivan, 21, died after being stabbed at the London beauty spot on 7 April.

headlines.title: Prince Harry says children should be an 'upgrade' of their parents
headlines.description: The Duke of Sussex combines two of his passions, mental health campaigning and sport, on the second day of an Australia visit.

headlines.title: 'Bit of pain' worth long-term security from Iran, Bessent tells BBC
headlines.description: US Treasury Secretary said a "small bit of economic pain" was worth it to eliminate the threat of Iranian strikes on Western capitals.

headlines.title: Tracking the ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Four vessels with links to Iran have crossed the Strait of Hormuz despite the start of a US naval blockade, but two of those vessels appear to have reversed course.

headlines.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports?
headlines.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

headlines.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
headlines.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

headlines.title: New search engine reveals if ancestors were in Nazi party
headlines.description: Christian Rainer told the BBC he found his grandfather within seconds using the online tool, which also helped clear other members of his family.

headlines.title: King not meeting Epstein survivors, but state visit could help ease US-UK tensions
headlines.description: The US state visit will see the King heading into choppy diplomatic waters during London-Washington tensions.

headlines.title: Don't feel like exercising? Maybe it's the wrong time of day for you
headlines.description: Time your workout to your body clock, health researchers advise based on latest evidence.

headlines.title: A cold could kill my daughter - hospital visits feel like a death sentence
headlines.description: Rebecca Quayle, who has terminal cancer, has had to wait in A&E with people coughing and taking Covid tests.

headlines.title: 'I'm not the same person': Victim of secret home filming speaks out about impact of voyeurism
headlines.description: Lucy Domaille was filmed by Kirk Bishop through a gap in the curtains of her home.

headlines.title: Stormzy's stab vest goes on display in landmark exhibition of black British music
headlines.description: The Banksy-designed vest features alongside artefacts from Shirley Bassey, Sade and Craig David.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'UK economy takes triple hit' and 'Cheers, Timmy!'
headlines.description: Iran war impact on UK economy and Chalamet's opera-ballet jab surges ticket sales leads the papers.

headlines.title: Ukraine's military to get biggest-ever shipment of UK drones
headlines.description: With attention on the Middle East, Defence Secretary John Healey said 'Putin wants us to be distracted.'

headlines.title: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
headlines.description: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

headlines.title: South Korea jails American YouTuber for public nuisance
headlines.description: Johnny Somali sparked outrage after kissing a statue representing World War Two sex slaves.

headlines.title: Man wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
headlines.description: "How do I know this isn't a prank?" the winner asked when he answered a video call from Christie's auction house in Paris.

headlines.title: Ultra-marathon champion dies while running trail in Scotland
headlines.description: David Parrish was trying to beat the record for the fastest man to complete the 234 mile trail.

headlines.title: 250 missing after migrant boat sinks in Indian Ocean
headlines.description: The trawler "reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding", the United Nations said.

headlines.title: World Cup winner Kildunne reveals body dysmorphia struggle
headlines.description: England rugby star Ellie Kildunne reveals she suffered from disordered eating and body dysmorphia during the coronavirus lockdown.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Can we afford to keep the UK safe?
headlines.description: Former Nato chief warns UK's national security 'in peril'.

headlines.title: Americanswers... on 5 Live! Donald Trump vs Pope Leo on Iran
headlines.description: The US president insults the pope and depicts himself as Jesus

headlines.title: Slot's Isak gamble backfires as Liverpool trophy hopes end
headlines.description: Arne Slot's gamble to start Alexander Isak against PSG backfires with his own future also in the balance after a Champions League exit.

headlines.title: Are Kompany's Bayern a glimpse at football after Guardiola?
headlines.description: With Vincent Kompany making an impressive impact at Bayern Munich, BBC Sport's tactics expert Umir Irfan takes a closer look at the manager's unique style.

headlines.title: World Cup winner Kildunne reveals body dysmorphia struggle
headlines.description: England rugby star Ellie Kildunne reveals she suffered from disordered eating and body dysmorphia during the coronavirus lockdown.

headlines.title: Slot fears Ekitike injury is 'really bad'
headlines.description: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says Hugo Ekitike's injury 'looks really bad' after the France striker was taken off on a stretcher during Tuesday's Champions League loss to Paris St-Germain.

headlines.title: Does it matter if Wrexham don't get promoted this season?
headlines.description: BBC Sport examines whether it matters if Wrexham don't seal promotion to the Premier League this season at the first time of asking.

headlines.title: Why Bellingham can rescue Real's Champions League dream
headlines.description: Jude Bellingham was behind Real Madrid's late resurgence against Bayern Munich last week in their Champions League first-leg tie, and could be key in Wednesday's return.

headlines.title: Peaty wins British 100m title in 'astonishing' time
headlines.description: Adam Peaty puts on an "astonishing" performance in the men's 100m breaststroke final, with a time of 58.97 seconds at the Acquatics Great British Swimming Championships.
